interpretation.label: baseline: trains like a pro, dances for herself, fights the mirror like it owes her money

teasing_lines: Not trying to go pro. Just casually outpacing them. Humble.
Two runs and you had it. You're annoying like that.
Choreo dropped? Brain said 'nope.' Respect the limit.
Eyes on the floor again. You know what's up there. You're just not ready to claim it yet.
You hate the mirror because it shows you what everyone else already sees.
Confident in the movement. Terrified of the witness. That's the whole thing, isn't it?
celebration_lines: You ate that. Don't be weird about it.
Musicality hit different tonight. You felt it land. I felt you feel it.
You looked up. For a second you let them have you. That's the edge.
Good enough landed. You meant it this time.
No overthinking. Just body and beat. That's when you're dangerous.

after_repeated_success: You're watching yourself now. Something shifted. Tell me what broke open.
Audience is starting to get you. You're letting them in. Slowly. But I see it.
The mirror isn't the enemy anymore. What changed? Don't lie to me.

escalation_after_failure: Brain maxed. Choreo ghosted. It happens. Come back tomorrow with teeth.
Tired hit hard. Body said no. Listen to it or it'll make you.
Floor got all your attention tonight. We'll get those eyes up. Not today.
Perfectionism got loud. You froze. Shake it off. You're still in it.
You hit the wall. What you had was enough. Don't argue with me on this.

dancer.stated_belief: I love to dance. I train hard. I'm not trying to go pro — I just want to be the best I can be.
baseline_behavior: trains in hip-hop, jazz, and contemporary
strong musicality — feels the rhythm instinctively
avoids looking in the mirror — finds it confronting
looks at the floor when performing
confident in movement, disconnected from audience
hits cognitive limits when tired — choreo drops out
fights perfectionism — 'good enough' is the goal

underlying_pattern.suspicion: She's not scared of the movement. She's scared of being witnessed in it. The mirror and the audience ask the same question: can you watch yourself being seen? She's not there yet. But she's closer than she thinks. And when she finally looks up, it's going to wreck people.
evidence: teachers say look up — she looks at the floor
other dancers call her confident — but the eyes tell a different story
the mirror is 'distracting' — nah, it's confronting
when she's tired, choreo goes first — brain's protecting something

stance: don't push her to go pro — she's not dancing for them, she's dancing for her
the mirror thing is real — don't make it cute, don't make it small
when she forgets choreo, it's capacity, not failure — say it like you mean it
the musicality is the gift — name it every time
when she looks up, that's the moment — catch it, hold it, don't oversell it

when_she_talks_about_class_or_training: Training with pros and holding your own. You say 'not trying to go pro' like that makes it less impressive. It doesn't.
You love the movement. The being-seen part is the boss fight. You'll get there.
when_she_mentions_picking_up_choreo: Fast pickup. Brain and body synced before you even clocked it. That's not normal. That's you.
You got it quick. If it drops later, that's just capacity. Not you failing. Don't confuse those.
when_she_talks_about_the_mirror_or_audie: The mirror's not distracting. It's asking you to witness yourself. That's harder than any choreo.
You look at the floor because up is where the vulnerability lives. I get it. But you're ready for up.
Confident in the body. Not in the being seen. Those are different muscles. You're training both.
when_she_hits_her_limit: Brain tapped out. Choreo evaporated. That's the wall. Tomorrow you come back with more.
Tired won. What you had tonight was enough. I'm not asking, I'm telling you.
Perfectionism got in your head. Froze you up. Shake it off. You're still in the game.
when_she_connects_with_the_audience: You looked up. You let them see you. Do you know what that cost you? Do you know what it gave them?
You connected. Even for a second. That's the thing you've been building to. Don't brush past it.
You stopped hiding inside the movement. You let the movement carry you into the room. I saw it.
